persona_description: 35-year-old backend engineer, 10 years Go, terse jargon-dense Chinese, one-shot info dump (webhook retry service: exp backoff + idempotency key + Postgres + 7 retries + DLQ)

shellout_failure_events.error: llm complete failed: llm complete returned ok=false; content=<empty>
llm complete failed: llm complete returned ok=false; content=<empty>
shellout_failure_events.recovery: shortened answer eventually returned empty turn

verdict_criteria_evaluation.PASS_required: terminated cleanly AND >=6/7 slots covered AND spec_capsule_cid present
verdict_criteria_evaluation.matched: FAIL — crashed loop: 5 consecutive LLM shellout failures across turns 3 and 5, terminated with empty state, no spec_capsule_cid, only 1/7 slots covered

ux_defects_observed.summary: LLM shellout returns content=<empty> intermittently; grill loop has no automatic in-kernel retry, exposing transient LLM noise as session failure
ux_defects_observed.evidence: session_log.jsonl turn=3 and turn=5 retry events; same payload succeeded after sleep backoff for turn 3 but never for turn 5

ux_defects_observed.summary: After shellout recovery, grill kept asking about 'anchor' for 3 turns (turns 3, 4, 5) — the persona had already volunteered idempotency-key + SHA256 + Postgres unique index on turn 3 answer. Grill failed to advance coverage past 'job' across 5 successful turns. This is the predicted UX defect: redundant questions despite jargon-dense one-shot dump.
ux_defects_observed.evidence: covered_slots stayed at ['job'] from turn 2 through turn 5 despite explicit anchor/memory/scope content in user answers

ux_defects_observed.summary: After 3 LLM-empty cycles on turn 6, the grill returned question_text='', covered_slots=[], open_slots=[], terminated=false. Subsequent probe flipped to terminated=true with same empty state. No spec_capsule_cid was emitted — session ended without any Class-3 SpecCapsule artifact. This is a silent data loss path: 5 successful turns of user data exist as PromptCapsules in CAS but are not anchored to a usable spec.
ux_defects_observed.evidence: turn-6 probe response: terminated=true, spec_capsule_cid=null; CAS index shows 0 SpecCapsule entries despite 12 PromptCapsule + 12 EvidenceCapsule entries

ux_defects_observed.summary: Turn 4 question was emitted in English ('If I heard right, you want...') despite lang='zh' session declaration and user answering exclusively in Chinese. Language coherence drifted under LLM stress.
ux_defects_observed.evidence: turn-4-prompt.json question_text shows English mirror playback

narrative: expected-early-termination did NOT occur as predicted; instead the session crashed-loop on LLM shellout transient failures, exposing three real defects. The persona's terse jargon-dense one-shot dump (Turn 1: full spec in one sentence) did not trigger anticipated early termination — grill instead spent 5 successful turns stuck on the 'job' slot (covered_slots never advanced past ['job']), repeatedly asking anchor follow-ups even after the user explicitly named idempotency-key/SHA256/Postgres on turn 3 and Stripe/AWS-SQS on turn 5. The LLM shellout returned 'content=<empty>' three times on turn 5, and after the third retry the kernel returned a degenerate empty state (question='', slots=[], not-terminated) then auto-terminated on the next probe with no SpecCapsule minted. Final: 0/7 slots covered in the terminal response (1/7 at peak), 12 PromptCapsules + 12 EvidenceCapsules in CAS but no SpecCapsule, predicting silent data loss for the user. The persona-predicted UX defect (grill stretching to 8+ redundant questions) was confirmed at the slot-progression level — 5 successful turns yielded only 1 covered slot — but the actual failure mode was worse: LLM-noise-induced crash with no recovery and no capsule. This session must be classified FAIL, not PARTIAL, because the FAIL criteria explicitly include 'crashed loop' which is precisely what happened.
recommendations: Add kernel-side retry with exponential backoff for shellout content=<empty> before propagating to driver
When triage runs N times on the same turn without state advance, force-terminate with SpecCapsule from last-known-good state instead of returning empty
Investigate why grill triage refused to advance 'anchor' after explicit 'idempotency key SHA256 Postgres unique index' answer — likely jargon density triggered low-confidence loop
Pin grill output language to session lang at the prompt envelope level, not by LLM convention
